text.title: Learning Speed as Competitive Moat Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Learning Speed as Competitive Moat plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: The org MUST maintain a versioned register of active product beliefs (assumption, confidence, source, last-updated); beliefs older than 90 days without update MUST be flagged.
rule.rationale: Implicit beliefs cannot be updated; without a register the org cannot tell whether it is learning or repeating yesterday's bets.

rule.statement: Every strategic decision MUST cite at least one outside-view input (competitor move, market data, customer signal) alongside any inside-view reasoning.
rule.rationale: Inside-view-only decisions cluster on the loudest voice; outside view is the structural counter to founder narrative gravity.

rule.statement: Every reversible decision MUST be logged in a decision journal with date, alternatives considered, expected outcome, and review date; review-date no-shows are a tracked metric.

rationale.source: Shane Parrish — Farnam Street decision journal

rule.rationale: Without a journal, hindsight bias rewrites past decisions to look smart; the journal is the only feedback loop for calibration.

rule.statement: Every product bet MUST have pre-registered kill criteria (signal threshold + observation window); bets without kill criteria are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without kill criteria, sunk-cost reasoning keeps bets alive past their evidence window; pre-registration is the only structural gate.

rule.statement: The org MUST hold a weekly 30-minute belief-update ritual where each bet's confidence is re-scored against the week's signals; missed weeks count against the moat.
rule.rationale: Beliefs that update on a yearly planning cycle are slower than competitor cycles; weekly is the minimum cadence to compound a learning moat.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
